title: Blaming Shinji for NTI and no one for Actual Third Impact
display_title: Blaming Shinji for NTI and no one for Actual Third Impact
wikitext:
[]: Blaming Shinji for NTI and no one for Actual Third Impact             Quote Post by AsukaShikinami10 » Sun Feb 23, 2025 2:13 am I don't know if there is already an existing topic of this subject, but I'd like to share my opinion about this. The reason why everyone seems to blame only Shinji for the Near Third Impact and no one else is because Near Third Impact and Actual Third Impact are the same event, only paused for a small period of time thanks to Kaworu and the Spear of Cassius. Even in 3.333 when Kaworu defriefs some light of the events transcurred, he said it was Shinji who cause Third Impact, and that Lilin call it Near Third Impact. The continuation of Third Impact happened because an Angel (the 12th one) made it to the bottommost part of Central Dogma by the means of Mark.06 and they had the urgency to pull out the spear from Unit 01's body; thus provoking Third Impact to resume were it was left. It was certainly not in Gendo's or SEELE's plans for the Angel to achieve its goal and kill Lilith (remember that in Thrice they used the 4 battleships to recreate Lilith by generating 12 wings of light and later Gendo used the Eva Imaginary as a replacement for Lilith). In 2.22 when Eva 01 pseudo-evolved into a radiant giant Gendo said the promised time was near, but then it all went to hell with the arrival of the last 2 remaining angels with no funcional Eva Units left; Kaworu was put aside by SEELE's command (maybe to ensure his later role as the 1st and last Angel) so they made Mark.06 an autonomous unit, and Eva 02 wasn't fully functional. So with all of this in mind I have no doubt now that the only one who really caused the Third Impact to begin with was Shinji and only him.

[]: Re: Blaming Shinji for NTI and no one for Actual Third Impact             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Feb 26, 2025 10:51 am First, it's not as if no one blames SEELE or Gendo - they're spoken of with abject disdain by any WILLE ppl who mention them. Second, you're looking for a rational explanation for what's fundamentally an emotional issue. Displaced aggression. It's a classic psychological defense mechanism. Everyone but Mari assumed that Shinji was as good as dead. It's safer to blame the dead guy than to agonize over your own unwitting contributions when you have to stay strong to keep shit from getting even worse. All the former NERV staff were useful pawns as much as Shinji was, he was only the final domino - heck, Ritsuko was with Gendo's faction & betrayed him. But if they focussed on that, they would be consumed by guilt or end up fighting & killing each other. So they blame the dead guy. He can't defend himself (but also, can't be hurt anymore, since he's dead.) (Asuka in particular seems to have trash-talked him to the younger members like Midori, maybe because spite helps her stay motivated and she hoped it would do the same for Midori.) And then, suddenly, he's not dead anymore, and has no clue what happened, a relic of the world that they lost forever and cannot help but rub in how much everything and everyone changing through his innocent ignorance. Also, the brief snippet that Shinji witnessed of WILLE in Q gave him a skewed idea of just how hated/blamed he truly is. Even within Q itself, Shinji doesn't witness Mari insinuating that Asuka may still harbor some attachment to him, never directly encounters Mari, who doesn't blame him at all, may have no idea that Misato could have offed him but couldn't bring herself to do it, or Fuyutsuki being like 'hey don't you wanna talk to him maybe?' at Gendo.

[]: Re: Blaming Shinji for NTI and no one for Actual Third Impact             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Feb 27, 2025 8:21 pm Kendrix wrote: Second, you're looking for a rational explanation for what's fundamentally an emotional issue.

[]: Re: Blaming Shinji for NTI and no one for Actual Third Impact             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sun Mar 02, 2025 9:48 am AsukaShikinami10 wrote: The continuation of Third Impact happened because an Angel (the 12th one) made it to the bottommost part of Central Dogma by the means of Mark.06 and they had the urgency to pull out the spear from Unit 01's body; thus provoking Third Impact to resume were it was left.
